steps.title: Use the circle points
steps.content: The plotted points define a circle, so the center must be the same distance from each of the three points. Once the center and radius are known, use $C=2\pi r$ to find the coefficient of $\pi$.

steps.title: Read the plotted points
steps.content: From the graph, the three points are $(2,6)$, $(6,10)$, and $(6,2)$. The points $(6,10)$ and $(6,2)$ are vertical endpoints with midpoint $(6,6)$, so any circle through both must have its center on the horizontal line through that midpoint.

steps.title: Check the common distance
steps.content: The point $(6,6)$ is $4$ units from $(6,10)$ and $4$ units from $(6,2)$. It is also $4$ units from $(2,6)$, since those points differ only in their $x$-coordinates. Because $(6,6)$ is equidistant from all three plotted points, it is the center of the circle, and the radius is $4$.

steps.title: Find the circumference coefficient
steps.content: Use the circumference formula with radius $4$: $$ C=2\pi r=2\pi(4)=8\pi $$ The circumference is written as $k\pi$, so the coefficient of $\pi$ is the requested value. The answer is 8.
